items.title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
items.summary: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.

items.title: Hugging Face CEO Thanks Chinese AI for Saving the Day After OpenAI Hack
items.summary: When American commercial AI refused to help investigate the breach, Hugging Face ran Chinese model GLM 5.2 locally. Its CEO now says there's an important lesson in this.

items.title: New Clarity Act emerges that's a start on the final draft, makes ethics rule temporary
items.summary: Lawmakers have circulated text of the latest draft that will be pushed toward a Senate finish line, but they're still debating a core government-ethics piece.

items.title: BitGo, OTC Markets plan tokenized securities access for broker-dealers
items.summary: The proposed alliance would enable more than 150 broker-dealers to trade and settle digital asset securities through OTC Link ATS using BitGo’s custody infrastructure.

items.title: Bitcoin price avoids major Iran jitters as S&P 500 ‘short squeeze’ on horizon
items.summary: Bitcoin and US stocks stayed strong in the face of US-Iran escalation as analysis predicted BTC price action outperforming equities later.

items.title: Revolut hits $115 billion valuation in employee share sale: WSJ
items.summary: The company reported strong 2025 performance, with a $2.3 billion pre-tax profit, $6 billion in revenue, and over 75 million customers.

items.summary: The commissioner said onchain vaults and lending strategies could resemble investment funds or advisers, depending on how they are structured.

items.title: UK digital bond plans hinge on one missing piece: onchain cash
items.summary: Experts say Britain's digital gilt pilot can unlock trapped liquidity in financial markets only if regulators approve an onchain stablecoin.

items.title: US Seizes More Than $25M in Crypto Tied to Investment and Romance Scams
items.summary: Prosecutors filed five forfeiture cases as Secret Service agents traced funds from thousands of victims to launderers in Southeast Asia.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: The crypto question isn't what to own — it's what you can survive holding
items.summary: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Lionsoul Global’s Gregory Mall writes that the primary crypto allocation decision is size, not selection.

items.title: Centralized Elements 'Frequently Persist' in DeFi and Should Be Regulated: FATF
items.summary: Nearly every country has yet to apply the rules, the watchdog said, warning of outright bans for platforms that don’t comply.

items.title: U.S. seeks forfeiture of $25 million in crypto tied to romance and investment scams
items.summary: The cases add to more than $800 million that authorities say a dedicated federal scam task force has recovered.

items.title: Forget Nvidia: The next big AI trade could be crypto and blockchain
items.summary: Franklin Templeton’s Sandy Kaul and Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire argue that as autonomous AI agents start spending money on their own, blockchain networks will power the next big AI trade.

items.title: The digital euro: Surveillance money, or a better alternative to cash?
items.summary: Proponents of the digital euro say its a better alternative to cash that’s fully backed by the ECB. But critics argue it could provide the EU with too much power over its citizens.

items.title: Franklin Templeton calls agentic AI next ‘killer’ use case for blockchain
items.summary: Franklin Templeton’s digital assets lead said that the autonomous AI agent economy will increase demand for blockchain protocols hosting machine-to-machine micropayments.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Justin Sun's HTX 'Rotating' On-Chain Wallets Amid UK Sanctions: TRM Labs
items.summary: TRM Labs says the UK-sanctioned exchange has rotated hot wallets across blockchains, leaving address-list screening struggling to keep pace.

items.title: Kalshi rolls out Midterm Hubs ahead of the November elections in the U.S.
items.summary: The prediction markets platform seeks to position itself as the primary source for election odds ahead of the midterm elections this year.

items.title: Bitcoin analysis eyes ‘serious volume’ after Binance sees 9K BTC daily outflow
items.summary: Binance set multimonth records for net BTC outflows on Tuesday, adding to an emerging trend of positive inflows to the US spot Bitcoin ETFs.

items.title: Morning Minute: OpenAI Model Escapes Containment, Hacks Hugging Face
items.summary: Plus, the BTC ETFs stay green. The Clarity Act hits a wall over who enforces the ethics rules. And Jack Mallers walks away from XXI Capital.

items.title: Zilliqa Ledger app vulnerability lets attackers recover signer’s private keys
items.summary: A security vulnerability in the Zilliqa Ledger app is enabling attackers to reconstruct private keys using publicly available onchain data.

items.title: Midnight token rebounds 19% after Wanchain bridge hack, Hoskinson calls for ZK revamp
items.summary: Charles Hoskinson says the exploit, which briefly sent NIGHT to an all-time low, underscores why the industry needs to move beyond legacy bridge infrastructure.

items.title: US seeks forfeiture of $25M in crypto tied to romance, investment scams
items.summary: US prosecutors are seeking more than $25 million in cryptocurrency allegedly connected to international fraud and laundering networks.

items.title: SecondFi to shut down after $2.4 million ADA wallet theft
items.summary: The breach stemmed from a vulnerability in transaction signing software that enabled the derivation of private keys from blockchain transaction data.

items.title: Jack Dorsey's Block Launches Buzz, a Nostr-Based Slack and GitHub Rival for AI Agents
items.summary: The free, open source workspace runs on Nostr, gives AI agents their own cryptographic identities, and challenges Slack and GitHub.

items.title: Talos brings institutional trading tools to Kalshi prediction markets
items.summary: The integration provides institutional clients with access to Kalshi’s event contracts and crypto perpetuals via Talos’ existing trading infrastructure.

items.title: Crypto institutions look beyond audits as trust signals falter: Hacken
items.summary: Institutional due diligence is shifting toward continuous monitoring, signer controls and incident readiness after operational failures accounted for most crypto losses.

items.title: South Korea crypto volumes shrink as retail investors shift to stocks
items.summary: Trading activity on South Korea’s five major crypto exchanges fell sharply as the KOSPI surged, signaling a shift in retail investors toward equities.

items.title: Bitcoin retreats from one-month high as oil tops $85, inflation concerns resurface
items.summary: BTC retreated from a one-month high as WTI crude topped $85 for the first time since June, reigniting inflation concerns and pushing investors toward gold, silver and the safety of bitcoin over altcoins.

items.summary: Payward's xStocks pushes beyond U.S. equities as competition to bring global stock markets onchain accelerates.

items.title: Foundry asks Bitcoin miners to vote on BIP-110 support
items.summary: Foundry USA asked its mining customers to signal their support for BIP-110, an actively debated proposal seeking to shrink the amount of data that can be stored in a Bitcoin transaction.

items.title: SecondFi to wind down after $2.6M ADA theft linked to wallet flaw
items.summary: SecondFi will wind down after a $2.6 million ADA theft, while users still await recovery tools that were initially expected within weeks of the exploit.

items.title: Galaxy pledges $5M for developers quantum-proofing Bitcoin
items.summary: Galaxy pledged up to $5 million in grants for developers working on Bitcoin’s quantum security and elected a council of quantum-advisory experts to research quantum-resistant migration solutions.

items.summary: An attacker fed the lending system a fake, abnormally low bitcoin price, liquidated vaults that should have been safe, and pocketed the difference in a single transaction.
